latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

As at the close of business on 30 June 2025, the final Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569
151%
As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group is a UK‑based investment company that owns and manages a small portfolio of residential and commercial properties. The firm buys, refurbishes and rents out properties, and it occasionally sells assets to raise capital for new projects.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK property market is one of the largest in Europe, and small, nimble operators can capture value in niche segments such as student housing or boutique retail. Allanfield’s focus on a concentrated portfolio gives it the flexibility to reposition assets quickly, which can create upside if market conditions improve.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in a building‑phase, raising capital and deploying it into properties. Revenue streams are modest and the firm has not yet reached a steady‑state cash flow. The share price is trading at a very low level, well below the value of its underlying assets, indicating a deep discount.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show the board is concentrating on returning capital to shareholders – a return of capital was announced in September 2025 and the AGM in December 2025 confirmed the strategy. The company is also updating its portfolio, listing a few new holdings and noting the sale of a small asset in March 2026.

Financially, the reports are thin – the half‑year statement from March 2025 shows a small operating loss, and the 2026 factsheet confirms the firm is still in a loss‑making phase but has improved its balance sheet by reducing debt.
Overall, the narrative is one of a company that is trying to stabilize its cash position while slowly building a more attractive asset base.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: There are no fresh bullish voices – the only recent chatter is old, neutral or mildly optimistic posts from 2016 and 2011 that are largely irrelevant to the current situation.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears would point to the lack of revenue, the thin operating loss and the fact that the share is trading at a fraction of its asset value, suggesting the company may still be too early in its life cycle to generate returns.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment has stayed flat over the last year – no new discussions, no trending topics, and no recent analyst upgrades.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning there is no public buzz. A sudden news event could catch the market off‑guard and trigger a sharp move.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming AGM in December 2025 could reveal a new capital‑raising plan or a shift in the asset strategy, which would be a clear sign of intent.
A sale of one of the newer properties listed in the March 2026 factsheet would inject cash and could be followed by a dividend or share buy‑back.
If the company announces a new development project or partnership in the next 90 days, that could lift investor confidence.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume – a 3‑fold jump over the 30‑day average – that would signal a breakout. Also monitor the price for a bounce to the 50‑day moving average, which would indicate the start of a recovery trend.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is still in the early phase of a deep crash. A mean‑reversion play would need a clear confirmation that capitulation is complete – e.g., sustained buying pressure and a break above a key support level.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the stock is indeed down 90%+ from its all‑time high, a rebound to just 50% of that peak would produce a 200% return on the original price. The deep discount suggests the market has overreacted, and the lack of any negative news makes a bounce plausible.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm is still loss‑making and has no proven revenue stream. If the company cannot raise additional capital or sell assets, the share price may stay depressed or decline further. The worst case is a continued slide to a price that reflects the book value of the assets.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could invest a modest amount at the current price, with the risk limited to that outlay. A 200% upside would be the reward if the share recovers to just half its peak value. The asymmetry is strong: small loss for a potentially large gain.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a break above the 50‑day moving average or on a volume‑spike that confirms the breakout. Use a small position size – 5–10% of your discretionary portfolio – and set a stop 10% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the AGM or a new asset sale announcement. Watch the price for a 20% rally from the current level; that would be a clear sign the market is taking notice.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no immediate delisting or bankruptcy signals. The only risk of a passing is that the company remains in a loss‑making phase and fails to raise capital.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Allanfield’s share is deeply discounted, sentiment is silent, and the only catalysts are upcoming corporate announcements; a contrarian play is justified if the company can raise capital or sell assets, offering a high upside with limited downside.
